name: California Casserole (1956)
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Boneless pork or veal, cut into one inch cubes
10 3/4 oz Condensed cream of chicken soup
10 3/4 oz Condensed cream of chicken soup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl or plastic bag, combine the flour and paprika; shake well.
2. Add pork/veal; coat well with the flour mixture.
3. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
4. Add meat; cook until browned.
5. Add salt, pepper, and water. Bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes or until meat is tender, stirring occasionally.
7. Transfer meat to an ungreased 13 x 9 (3-quart) baking dish.
8. In the same skillet, combine the condensed cream of chicken soup and water; bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
9. Pour over veal mixture in the baking dish.
10. Add onions; mix well.
11. Preheat oven to 425°F.
12. Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup; level off.
13. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, poppy seeds, minced onion, celery seed, poultry seasoning, and salt; mix well.
14. Add oil and enough milk so that the dry ingredients are just moistened when stirred.
15. In a small bowl, combine butter and bread crumbs.
16. Drop rounded tablespoons of dough into crumb mixture; roll to coat well.
17. Arrange dumplings over warm meat mixture.
18. Bake at 425°F for 20 to 25 minutes until dumplings are deep golden brown.
19. Meanwhile, in a medium saucepan, combine all sauce ingredients. Bring just to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 2 to 3 minutes or until thoroughly heated, stirring frequently.
20. Serve sauce with casserole and dumplings.
